vocab.meaning: giảm thiểu các rủi ro tài chính
vocab.example: By diversifying their investments through bank-led portfolios, individuals can effectively mitigate financial risks.

vocab.meaning: các công cụ/dịch vụ cho vay tín dụng
vocab.example: Many entrepreneurs rely on banks to provide essential credit facilities that allow them to scale their businesses.

vocab.meaning: trách nhiệm ủy thác (trách nhiệm pháp lý bảo vệ tiền cho khách hàng)
vocab.example: Banks have a fiduciary duty to act in the best interest of their clients when managing their life savings.

vocab.meaning: quản lý khả năng thanh khoản (tiền mặt sẵn có)
vocab.example: Banks are indispensable for effective liquidity management, ensuring that cash is available whenever a sudden need arises.

vocab.meaning: dịch vụ quản lý tài sản/thịnh vượng
vocab.example: High-net-worth individuals often utilize banks for their bespoke wealth management services to optimize their long-term returns.

vocab.meaning: các giao thức bảo mật nghiêm ngặt
vocab.example: The shift to digital banking has forced institutions to implement stringent security protocols to thwart cybercriminals.

vocab.meaning: sự minh bạch về tài chính
vocab.example: Using a bank account promotes fiscal transparency, as every single transaction is recorded and can be audited if necessary.

vocab.meaning: tối ưu hóa/hợp lý hóa việc chi trả lương
vocab.example: Most corporations use banking networks to streamline payroll, ensuring employees receive their wages accurately and on time.
People use banks to leverage their existing assets, such as a house, as collateral for obtaining significant personal loans.

vocab.meaning: kiềm chế lạm phát (thông qua các chính sách của ngân hàng trung ương)
vocab.example: From a macro perspective, banks are the tools through which central authorities raise interest rates to curb inflation.
